rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (3/10) and rest on the same substantive reasoning: the 'Basic' German proficiency failing the mandatory C1 requirement, the absence of formal people-management experience, and the lack of client-facing advisory work. None of the concerns or score movement keys off the location change from Berlin/various cities to India — the differences between the two writeups are mere rewording and ordinary run-to-run phrasing noise. The German C1 knockout is a legitimate job requirement applied identically in both versions.
